A meeting is defined as any encounter where both listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Who will Trump meet with in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual meets with Donald Trump between January 1, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's confirmed state visit to Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping on May 14-15 drives overwhelming trader consensus toward that outcome, as credible reporting verifies the in-person meeting delayed from earlier due to the Iran conflict and covering trade tariffs, artificial intelligence, Taiwan Strait tensions, and nuclear issues; CEOs from Tesla, Apple, Meta, and Boeing will accompany him. Earlier in 2026, Trump hosted Latin American leaders at the March Shield of the Americas security summit in Florida, while U.S. invitations extend to Vladimir Putin for the G20 in Miami later this year amid Ukraine dynamics. No verified meetings have occurred with Kim Jong Un or Pope Leo XIV, with lower probabilities reflecting diplomatic barriers and institutional protocols; upcoming G7 summit in June and midterms could prompt further bilateral engagements before year-end resolution.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 10°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Lowest temperature in London on May 13?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest UK Met Office and BBC forecasts for Heathrow—London's official temperature station—indicate an overnight minimum around 6-7°C on May 13 amid light showers, blustery westerly winds up to 38 mph gusts, and variable cloud cover, driving trader sentiment toward lower outcomes like 6°C (13.5%) and 7°C (20%). The closely matched probabilities across 7-12°C reflect ensemble model uncertainty, where thicker clouds from showers could trap heat and prevent drops below 7°C, while intermittent clears allow radiative cooling to 6°C or lower; brisk winds moderate extremes by mixing air layers. Historical May minima average 9°C, but the current cool, unsettled Atlantic pattern caps daytime highs at 15-16°C. New ECMWF and GFS runs later today may sharpen guidance ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Michael Echols be the Republican nominee for LA-05?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the LA-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 16, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: State Sen. Blake Miguez leads trader consensus at 51.5% implied probability to win Louisiana's 5th Congressional District Republican primary on May 16, driven by his dominant fundraising—nearly $4 million cash-on-hand reported mid-April, tripling challenger Michael Echols' $1.4 million—and President Trump's early endorsement. A Rainey Center poll from early April showed Miguez at 23%, Echols at 20%, Rick Edmonds at 10%, and 42% undecided among 889 likely voters, highlighting a fluid two-man race amid Miguez's out-of-district residency concerns that deter 42% of respondents. With the vote five days away, Miguez's resources position him to consolidate support in the open seat race vacated by Rep. Julia Letlow's Senate bid, while Echols trails at 22.4% on stronger favorability.

markets.question: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/6b1814d64903461b98144a6cc25eb79c.

, including both the chart and downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid escalating Middle East tensions from the ongoing Iran conflict—including Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure since early March 2026 that propelled Brent crude above $120 per barrel—Houthi rebels have repeatedly threatened to shutter the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a chokepoint handling 12% of global trade and key oil tanker routes. Recent warnings from Houthi officials in April underscore potential retaliation against U.S. naval actions, sustaining elevated war-risk insurance premiums and forcing major carriers like Maersk to reroute around Africa, adding $3–3.6 million in annual costs per vessel and inflating Asia-Europe freight rates. Trader consensus reflects low near-term closure risk, with transit volumes per IMF PortWatch data holding above effective blockage thresholds, though dual-chokepoint fears amplify oil supply volatility. Upcoming U.S. policy signals and coalition strikes could sway dynamics ahead of summer peak shipping season.

markets.question: Will Nikolai Denkov win the next Bulgarian presidential election?
markets.description:
Presidential elections in Bulgaria are expected to be held in the Fall of 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next presidential election in Bulgaria. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bulgarian government, specifically the Central Election Commission (Centralna izbiratelna komisia, CEC) (www.cik.bg/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent President Iliana Iotova leads trader consensus at 58% implied probability for the autumn 2026 Bulgarian presidential election, bolstered by her alignment with Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria party, which secured an absolute parliamentary majority in the April 19 snap election—its first since 1997—ending prolonged instability after eight votes in five years. Iotova granted Radev the first exploratory mandate to form a government on May 7, enhancing bloc momentum and her incumbency edge in early polls showing strong second-choice support from Radev sympathizers. Nikolai Denkov (11.5%) and Sofia Mayor Vassil Terziev (9.8%), both from the centrist We Continue the Change-Democratic Bulgaria alliance that garnered just 12-13% parliamentarily, trail amid fragmented opposition, while GERB's Rosen Zhelyazkov (4.5%) reflects weaker center-right positioning. The two-round system favors frontrunners, but opposition consolidation could challenge in a potential runoff.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes leads trader consensus for constructor pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix, driven by their commanding 2026 constructors' championship position with 180 points and top drivers George Russell and Andrea Kimi Antonelli showcasing superior one-lap qualifying pace throughout the season. Recent Miami Grand Prix developments saw McLaren's major upgrades deliver a sprint 1-2 for Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri, boosting their 27% implied probability amid strong low-speed traction on street-like circuits like Circuit Gilles Villeneuve, but Piastri cautioned on Mercedes' substantial Canada-specific upgrade package poised to counter. Red Bull and Ferrari trail at 15.5% and 13.5% respectively, hampered by inconsistent qualifying form and larger points deficits, while midfield teams like Williams and Haas linger below 5% absent recent breakthroughs. Track history favors Mercedes' aero efficiency on Montreal's high-speed straights and chicanes, with sprint qualifying on Saturday amplifying setup volatility.

markets.question: NBA Playoffs: Cavaliers vs. Pistons Total Games O/U 5.5
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Over” if more than 5.5 games are played during the 2026 NBA Playoffs Conference Semifinals series between the Cleveland Cavaliers and Detroit Pistons. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Under”.

If a partial series is played and not completed by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve according to the number of games played.

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the total number of games cannot be determined within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Cavaliers holding a 2-1 series lead over the Pistons in the Eastern Conference Semifinals after a gritty 116-109 Game 3 win on May 9—fueled by Donovan Mitchell's 35 points and James Harden's clutch play—traders heavily favor Over 5.5 total games at 73.5% implied probability, reflecting the series' back-and-forth intensity with no blowouts. Close contests, including Detroit's 107-97 Game 2 upset on the road, underscore matchup parity, bolstered by Cade Cunningham's playoff-leading scoring (302 points) and both teams' robust frontcourts featuring Evan Mobley, Jarrett Allen, and Karl-Anthony Towns. Minor injury concerns—Pistons' Kevin Huerter and Cavaliers' Sam Merrill questionable—haven't derailed stars, while Detroit's home-court edge in Game 4 today and first-round comeback resilience signal potential for six or seven games amid high-stakes playoff momentum.

markets.question: Will Lynn Vision win IEM Cologne Major 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the IEM Cologne 2026 tournament, currently scheduled for June 2nd - June 21st, 2026.

If this tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a winner has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizer, ESL (https://pro.eslgaming.com/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality leads trader consensus at 59.5% implied probability as the #1 HLTV-ranked squad and top seed with a direct invite to Stage 3 Swiss in the IEM Cologne Major 2026, bolstered by their dominant 2026 form including a win streak at IEM Atlanta and a BLAST Rivals Spring upper bracket final run past FUT Esports. Falcons sit at 10% as the #5-ranked team and Stage 3 invite, fueled by recent PGL Astana 2026 highlights like victories over Monte and 9z amid ongoing playoffs. Team Spirit's 7.5% reflects #6 HLTV standing and Stage 2 entry despite a prior IEM Rio loss to Falcons, while NaVi (5.1%) and FURIA (4.3%) benefit from top-3 ranks and Stage 3 paths. Odds align with Valve standings seeding, roster locks since April, and no major disruptions in the final pre-Major stretch.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.
markets.groupItemTitle: Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices United Russia at 96% to secure the most seats in Russia's September 18–20, 2026, State Duma election, reflecting its institutional dominance in the parallel voting system of 225 proportional party-list seats (5% threshold) and 225 single-member districts. Recent April polls from FOM and WCIOM show United Russia leading list vote intentions at 27–52%, far ahead of systemic opposition like KPRF (9–14%), LDPR (10–14%), New People (6–17%), and others, amid Kremlin-controlled processes. Ongoing United Russia primaries (ending May 31 online voting), featuring 17% war veterans and full contestation of Moscow districts, plus candidate announcements for occupied Ukrainian territories, reinforce expectations of retaining its current 315-seat supermajority. Upsets would require unprecedented disruptions like economic collapse or elite fractures, though opposition arrests underscore tight controls.

markets.question: Will Lighter reach $4 before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Lighter 1-minute candle for Lighter (LIT/USDC) between December 30, 2025, 14:25 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 ET has a final “High” price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Lighter, specifically the LIT/USDC “High” prices available at:
https://app.lighter.xyz/trade/LIT_USDC
with the chart set to “1m” (one-minute candles) on the top bar.

Only the Lighter LIT/USDC price feed will be used.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Erling Haaland's commanding 98% implied probability in the Premier League Golden Boot race stems from his league-leading 26 goals, a four-goal cushion over Brentford's Igor Thiago (22 goals) with only three matchdays remaining as of May 11, 2026. Haaland's prolific form—scoring in four of Manchester City's last five fixtures amid their title push—has widened the gap, bolstered by his elite expected goals (xG) of 25.2 and consistent touches in the opposition box. Thiago's recent hat-trick threat keeps faint hope alive at 1.3%, but traders price in the unlikelihood of him needing nine goals while Haaland scores zero. Realistic challenges include Haaland injury, suspension, or rest in dead-rubber games, though City's home finale versus Aston Villa favors his involvement.

markets.question: Will Tesla (TSLA) finish week of May 11 above $410?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

markets.question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a low near-term US-Iran diplomatic meeting amid a deepening stalemate in negotiations to solidify the fragile ceasefire following the 2026 conflict. President Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal on May 11 as "totally unacceptable," with Tehran demanding sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and war reparations—claims dismissed by Washington—while calling its offer "reasonable and generous." No direct talks have occurred in the past 30 days despite backchannel diplomacy via Qatar, Pakistan, and China, including Iran's foreign minister's recent Beijing visit. Incidents testing the Hormuz truce, like vessel seizures, heighten tensions. Upcoming Trump-Xi summit on May 14-15 could catalyze progress, though hardened positions on sanctions and nuclear issues persist.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no announcement of a permanent Attorney General nominee by June 30 at 50%, reflecting over five weeks since President Trump's April 2 dismissal of Pam Bondi, with Todd Blanche continuing seamlessly as acting AG without a Senate confirmation process. This positioning stems from reports of potential GOP Senate hurdles, such as from Sen. Thom Tillis, and Trump's historical preference for extended acting officials to bypass scrutiny. Lee Zeldin leads alternatives at 13% amid speculation of his strong DOJ alignment from his EPA tenure; Harmeet Dhillon (9.2%) and Blanche himself (8%) gain from legal loyalty to Trump, while Ken Paxton's 7.2% reflects Texas AG endorsement buzz. Absent a surprise nomination soon, markets anticipate delay into summer.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, National Assembly elections under proportional representation, no party secured a majority in the 90-seat chamber, with Robert Golob's liberal Freedom Movement (GS) taking 29 seats and Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28, trailed by NSi-SLS-Fokus (9), Democrats (6), Social Democrats (6), and Resni.ca (5). Golob's broad coalition bid collapsed on April 20, prompting his party to enter opposition, while President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate on April 25 due to insufficient support for any candidate reaching 46 votes. SDS-led talks with NSi-SLS-Fokus, Democrats, and Resni.ca stalled by May 6 amid Resni.ca's refusal to back either major leader, raising snap election risks if parliament fails to elect a PM nominee soon. Trader consensus reflects this hung parliament uncertainty, hinging on coalition breakthroughs or dissolution.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 73.5% on U.S. enactment of an AI safety bill before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress in the 119th Congress despite over 100 AI-related bills introduced since January 2025. None have advanced past committee amid partisan divides on regulatory scope—Democrats push stringent safety measures while Republicans prioritize innovation and preemption of state laws. The Trump administration's March 2026 National Policy Framework advocated light-touch federal standards, but it yielded no floor votes. Recent state actions, like Connecticut's comprehensive SB5 passing May 1 and Iowa's chatbot safety law signed days ago, underscore the patchwork approach filling the federal void. Approaching November 2026 midterms further dim passage prospects before year-end.

markets.question: Pigato vs. Bronzetti: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lisa Pigato and Lucia Bronzetti in the Parma, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Parma: Lisa Pigato vs Lucia Bronzetti Match O/U 22.5

events.title: Parma: Lisa Pigato vs Lucia Bronzetti
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lisa Pigato and Lucia Bronzetti in the Parma, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Lisa Pigato' if Lisa Pigato advances against Lucia Bronzetti.

This market will resolve to 'Lucia Bronzetti' if Lucia Bronzetti advances against Lisa Pigato.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the WTA 125 Parma Ladies Open first-round clash on clay, higher-ranked Italian Lisa Pigato (No. 141, 27-7 YTD) faces compatriot Lucia Bronzetti (No. 173, 8-12) in an all-azzurri derby boosted by recent wildcards. Pigato enters with momentum from clay challenger deep runs, including a QF win over Lina Gjorcheska last month, and holds a 1-0 H2H edge from their 2019 ITF clay meeting. Bronzetti, a surface specialist rebuilding after a rankings slide from peak No. 46, advanced in Oeiras but fell early in Rome to McCartney Kessler. No injuries reported; home crowd and rest from recent clay action could sway this competitive matchup.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.title: How high will inflation get in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on peak 2026 inflation hinges on the recent acceleration in price pressures, with March 2026 CPI rising to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—driven by energy surges and sticky core components at 0.2% month-over-month. The Federal Reserve's March Summary of Economic Projections lifted its median PCE inflation forecast for the year to 2.7% from 2.4%, reflecting robust labor markets and upside risks from tariffs and supply constraints, while core PCE hit 3.2% in March. Tomorrow's April CPI release, consensus at 3.7% headline and 2.7% core, looms as a pivotal catalyst, alongside May 28 PCE data and the June FOMC meeting, where markets price limited rate cuts amid persistent inflation above the 2% target.

markets.question: Will Abdul El-Sayed win the 2026 Michigan Democratic Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Abdul El-Sayed at 52.5% implied probability to win Michigan's Democratic U.S. Senate primary on August 4, driven by his strong performance among voters under 40 in April Emerson polling, robust Q1 fundraising that outpaced rivals, and grassroots momentum in a crowded field. State Sen. Mallory McMorrow follows at 28.5% after tying El-Sayed in recent surveys, though a May 3 controversy over deleted social media posts may have tempered gains. Rep. Haley Stevens trails at 18.7% despite a May 6 endorsement from former Sen. Debbie Stabenow and a $5 million ad buy this week from an AIPAC-linked super PAC, highlighting external efforts to consolidate establishment support amid a competitive three-way race.

markets.question: Bengaluru 2: Ronit Karki vs Manish Sureshkumar
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Ronit Karki and Manish Sureshkumar in the Bengaluru 2, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Ronit Karki' if Ronit Karki advances against Manish Sureshkumar.

This market will resolve to 'Manish Sureshkumar' if Manish Sureshkumar advances against Ronit Karki.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Young American qualifier Ronit Karki, ranked No. 962, carries momentum into his first-round Bengaluru 2 Challenger clash against higher-ranked Indian No. 829 Manish Sureshkumar on outdoor hard courts, marking their head-to-head debut. The 18-year-old Karki dominated qualifying this week with straight-set wins over Aradhya Kshitij (6-3, 6-2) and Parth Aggarwal (6-2, 6-1), extending his 2026 hard court record to 10-4 following a M25 Las Vegas final. Sureshkumar, 26 and a career 173-140 on hard, benefits from home conditions and crowd support but snapped a four-match win streak with a tight second-round exit at ITF M25 Nakhon Pathom last week. No injury concerns reported ahead of the May 12 matchup.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding victory in New Jersey's 11th congressional district special election on April 16, vacated by Mikie Sherrill's gubernatorial win, drives trader consensus on a 20-25% margin of victory, aligning with partial results showing her lead at roughly 20 points with 95% of votes counted. Strong Democratic turnout in early voting and mail ballots—61% Democratic versus 25% Republican—signaled dominance pre-election, overcoming the district's moderate history and pockets of resistance in Jewish-heavy towns like Livingston over her progressive stances. Final certification awaits outstanding provisionals and absentees, but slim odds on narrower margins reflect minimal risk of shifts amid historical special election Democratic overperformance. Late-breaking recounts or irregularities could theoretically challenge this, though evidence points to stability.

markets.question: Deandre Ayton: Points O/U 9.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 11 at 10:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Deandre Ayton scores more than 9.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Deandre Ayton scores 9.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Thunder vs. Lakers
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 11 at 10:30PM ET:
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 3-0 series lead over the Los Angeles Lakers in the Western Conference semifinals stems from dominant playoff performances, including Saturday's 131-108 Game 3 rout at Crypto.com Arena that extended OKC's winning streak to six games. Despite Jalen Williams sidelined by a recurring hamstring strain and Thomas Sorber out with a torn ACL, Chet Holmgren's 24 points and 12 rebounds highlighted Thunder depth. Lakers lack Luka Doncic (left hamstring, no return timetable) and list Jarred Vanderbilt as doubtful (finger), fueling trader consensus toward OKC ahead of elimination Game 4 tonight in LA, where home-court and desperation could spark an upset amid LA's back-to-back losses.

markets.question: FK Spartak Moskva vs. FK Rubin Kazan: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Russian Premier League game between FK Spartak Moskva and FK Rubin Kazan, scheduled for May 11 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FK Spartak Moskva and FK Rubin Kazan combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierliga.ru. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FK Spartak Moskva vs. FK Rubin Kazan - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Russian Premier League game, scheduled for May 11 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spartak Moscow host Rubin Kazan in a pivotal late-season Russian Premier League clash at Lukoil Arena, with Spartak sitting 6th on 42 points from 24 matches (GD +6) and Rubin 8th on 34 points (GD -2), both vying for European spots amid a tight mid-table battle. Spartak's recent form shows resilience with a 1-1 draw at Rostov last weekend following wins over Lokomotiv and Pari NN, bolstered by home advantage and a dominant head-to-head record, including a 2-0 away win over Rubin last August. Rubin drew 0-0 at Orenburg on April 11 but struggle away (mixed form, defensive setup under coach Franc Artiga since January), with long-term absences like Iwu (knee) potentially limiting midfield depth; no major new injuries reported for either side as lineups firm up.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's May 1 announcement directing the Pentagon to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months has heightened NATO tensions, with allies seeking clarification on impacted operations and bracing for potential deeper cuts in Spain and Italy. This move underscores ongoing US pressure on European partners for greater burden-sharing amid criticisms of insufficient defense spending. However, full US withdrawal from NATO faces steep legal hurdles under a 2023 congressional law prohibiting unilateral presidential action without two-thirds Senate approval, plus a one-year notice period per Article 13. No formal exit process has begun, maintaining US membership status. Traders eye upcoming NATO summits and congressional responses for shifts in alliance dynamics.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wuhan be 27°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

markets.question: Will LeBron James play for the Los Angeles Lakers in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team LeBron James officially joins by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If LeBron James does not officially join a new team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Los Angeles Lakers”.

If LeBron James joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If LeBron James retires or is not under contract with any professional team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official acquisition announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the Los Angeles Lakers and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

markets.question: Spread: Sport Lisboa e Benfica (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 11 at 3:15 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Sport Lisboa e Benfica" if Sport Lisboa e Benfica win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "SC Braga".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Benfica welcomes fourth-placed SC Braga to Estádio da Luz on May 10 in a pivotal Primeira Liga matchday clash, with the hosts in second chasing leaders FC Porto amid tight title and European qualification battles. Braga grapples with key absences, including star Ricardo Horta sidelined by a mid-May muscle injury, Victor Gómez out with a hand issue until late May, plus Florian Grillitsch (knock) and B. Arrey-Mbi doubtful after late-April setbacks that have strained their defense. Benfica boasts cleaner injury news and leverages superior home form plus historical head-to-head edge (36 wins to Braga's 15), though recent draws like December's 2-2 and Braga's January League Cup triumph add intrigue to this end-of-season decider.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the best Math AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Math" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Math" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/math-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Tottenham Hotspur languishing in 17th place after 35 Premier League matches and winless in league play this calendar year, trader sentiment for total corners against 14th-placed Leeds United hinges on Spurs' home games averaging 11.1 total corners this season, fueled by their high-pressing style despite a mounting injury crisis—captain Cristian Romero out for the season with a knee issue, plus eight others sidelined or doubtful. Leeds' away fixtures average 8.8 corners, with the visitors conceding 4.7 per game amid their own absences like Gabriel Gudmundsson's hamstring injury. Recent coalition talks collapsed after over six weeks, with King Frederik X tasking Venstre leader Troels Lund Poulsen as formateur on May 8—elevating his odds to 17.6%—after Moderates chief Lars Løkke Rasmussen withdrew support and nominated him, positioning Rasmussen at 9.7%. markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 115-139 tweets from May 11 to May 13, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 11 12:00 PM ET to May 13, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With incumbent President Emmanuel Macron constitutionally barred from a third consecutive term, France's 2027 presidential election—first round April 11 or 18, second round two weeks later—features a crowded, fragmented field requiring 500 parrainages from elected officials across at least 30 departments for ballot access by early March. Hard-left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 3 announcement of his fourth candidacy exacerbates left-wing divisions ahead of a contested October 11 primary, while National Rally's lineup hinges on Marine Le Pen's July 7 appeals court ruling on her embezzlement conviction's five-year ineligibility ban, potentially paving the way for Jordan Bardella, who leads first-round polls at 35%. Centrists Gabriel Attal and Édouard Philippe are positioning aggressively amid ongoing hung parliament instability under Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service forecasts for San Francisco's May 12 high temperature, with models clustering around 62–66°F due to persistent marine stratus from cool Pacific sea surface temperatures near 55°F and west-southwest winds of 10–20 mph limiting coastal warming. Recent NWS updates show partial afternoon clearing after overnight lows near 52–53°F, boosting the 66°F or higher implied probability to 33.5% as some guidance like GFS hints at earlier burn-off and subsidence from a building high-pressure ridge; however, ECMWF leans cooler with prolonged clouds favoring 62–65°F outcomes. Key differentiator is boundary layer mixing—stronger onshore flow caps peaks at 62–63°F (27%), while lighter winds enable 64–65°F (28.5%). Watch this afternoon's forecast discussion from the NWS San Francisco Bay Area office for model shifts ahead of peak heating around 2–4 p.m.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 180-199 Truth Social posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

markets.question: Will Annie January (Starlight) die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Independiente Santa Fe holds trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability for victory over CA Platense in Copa Libertadores Group E, propelled by home advantage at high-altitude Estadio Nemesio Camacho El Campín, where visiting teams from sea-level Buenos Aires often struggle with acclimatization. Santa Fe's recent 1-1 draws versus Corinthians and América de Cali underscore a resilient but goalless Group E campaign (third place), though key defensive injuries to Emmanuel Olivera (quadriceps muscle) and Helibelton Palacios (hamstring), reported May 9, raise concerns over backline stability. Platense enters as competitive underdogs at 25% after their 2-1 home win last month and solid form, keeping draw probabilities elevated at 28% amid Santa Fe's drawing streak.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly prices no Trump-Putin meeting by June 30 at 91.6%, reflecting the absence of any announced bilateral summit or diplomatic venue amid stalled Ukraine peace efforts. A U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, effective May 9-11, collapsed today with mutual accusations of violations, reinforcing the conflict's entrenched stalemate despite recent Trump-Putin phone calls on de-escalation. The White House confirms no plans in the immediate future, with Trump's focus on an upcoming Xi summit in Beijing and Putin's separate China visit. Prior summits, like the 2025 Alaska meeting, required extensive preparation; a rapid shift would demand a Ukraine breakthrough, new crisis such as Iran escalation, or urgent negotiations.

markets.question: Darderi vs. Zverev: Match O/U 23.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Luciano Darderi and Alexander Zverev in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Luciano Darderi vs Alexander Zverev Match O/U 23.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Luciano Darderi vs Alexander Zverev
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Luciano Darderi and Alexander Zverev in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Luciano Darderi' if Luciano Darderi advances against Alexander Zverev.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Zverev' if Alexander Zverev advances against Luciano Darderi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Second-seeded Alexander Zverev, a two-time Internazionali BNL d'Italia champion on clay, advanced to the round of 16 with a dominant 6-1, 6-4 win over Alexander Blockx on Sunday—their second meeting in nine days after Zverev's Madrid semifinals triumph. Luciano Darderi, the 18th seed and home favorite, earned his first Masters 1000 last-16 berth via a gritty comeback, rallying from a set and break down to defeat Tommy Paul 3-6, 6-3, 6-2. Zverev leads their head-to-head 1-0, including a straight-sets Rome third-round victory in 2024, bolstered by his Foro Italico expertise and top-tier clay form. Darderi's ongoing clay surge—highlighted by his Santiago title and multiple 2026 trophies—fuels upset potential amid home-crowd support and no reported injuries.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by 0.4% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders have priced April 2026 CPI month-over-month at 0.6% (32.5% implied probability) just ahead of 0.5% (30%) and 0.7% (26.5%), reflecting a razor-thin consensus for modest moderation from March's unexpectedly hot 0.9% print, which was propelled by energy price spikes amid Iran-related geopolitical tensions. Core CPI's steadier 0.2% advance in March, coupled with stabilizing oil after a sharp 22% drop and lagging shelter costs, fuels the tight race, as bettors weigh Wall Street previews clustering at 0.6-0.7% against potential upside from airline fares and used vehicles. With the Bureau of Labor Statistics release tomorrow at 8:30 a.m. ET, this skin-in-the-game sentiment underscores key swing factors like volatile energy components ahead of FOMC policy implications.

eventMetadata.context_description: Unics Kazan enters the VTB United League semi-final opener with home-court advantage at Basket-Hall, bolstering trader consensus at 58.5% implied probability amid their superior regular-season mark of 33-7 versus Zenit St. Petersburg's 28-12. Unics dominated quarterfinals, sweeping MBA-MAI 3-0 with strong rebounding led by Marcus Bingham (15.5 PPG, 7.2 RPG) and Jalen Reynolds (18.6 PPG), while Zenit advanced 3-1 over Uralmash, highlighted by a road 78-61 clincher. Recent head-to-head favors Zenit (86-69 win April 15), but no major injuries reported—watch Paris Lee and Reynolds for Unics, Luka Šamanić for Zenit—keeping the matchup competitive with Unics' playoff momentum tilting odds.

markets.question: Spread: Athletic Club (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Athletic Club" if Athletic Club win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "RCD Espanyol de Barcelona".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: RCD Espanyol de Barcelona vs. Athletic Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: RCD Espanyol hosts Athletic Club at RCDE Stadium on May 13 in a pivotal La Liga clash, with both sides entangled in the lower mid-table scrap where points could prove decisive against relegation. Espanyol's trader sentiment is weighed down by a dismal winless streak spanning all of 2026—last victory a 2-1 upset at San Mamés against Athletic on December 22, 2025—managing just six points from 48 possible amid a grueling run-in facing Real Madrid, Sevilla, Osasuna, and Real Sociedad. Athletic, sitting slightly higher around 10th-12th with 38-41 points after 33 matches, boasts steadier recent form but grapples with lingering concerns over Nico Williams' groin recovery. Head-to-head favors the hosts lately, yet Espanyol's home form and desperation clash against Athletic's away resilience, with no major injuries reported in the past week.

markets.question: Will Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Hims & Hers Health is estimated to release earnings on May 11, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Hims & Hers Health's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.03 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hims & Hers Health reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.03 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Hims & Hers Health releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.
